question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an unprivileged OPERATOR who holds a coarse-grained isAuthorized[maker][operator]=true grant on Midnight call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for a Merkle root containing offers the maker never intended to publish, under PRECONDITIONS: operator was authorized by maker for an unrelated purpose (e.g., collateral management), maker has authorized SetterRatifier as ratifier in Midnight, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: operator calls setIsRootRatifier(maker, root, true) then any taker calls midnight.take(offer, abi.encode(root, leafIndex, proof), units, taker, ...), violating INVARIANT: ratifier approval must bind correct maker intent and offer terms, causing scoped impact: unauthorized borrowing or credit creation against maker's assets? Proof idea: unit test where operator != maker, isAuthorized[maker][operator]=true, operator ratifies root containing high-maxUnits sell offer, taker fills it, assert maker's debt increases without maker's direct consent.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified + Midnight.take] Can an OPERATOR who is both authorized by maker (isAuthorized[maker][operator]=true) and acts as taker perform a self-dealing attack under PRECONDITIONS: operator calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for a root containing a sell offer (maker=maker, buy=false), then operator calls midnight.take(offer, ratifierData, units, operator, ...) as taker, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: operator.setIsRootRatified -> operator.take, violating INVARIANT: gates, ratifiers, and authorizations must not be bypassable; maker must consent to each offer fill, causing scoped impact: maker forced into debt position without signing or explicitly publishing the offer? Proof idea: fuzz operator address, assert maker.debt increases and operator receives loan assets without maker's direct signature.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an AUTHORIZED OPERATOR exploit the absence of permanent cancellation in SetterRatifier (unlike EcrecoverRatifier.cancelRoot which is irreversible) to re-enable a previously revoked root under PRECONDITIONS: maker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) to revoke, operator calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) to re-enable, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: maker.revoke -> operator.re-enable -> taker.take, violating INVARIANT: cancelled or invalid offers cannot be filled, causing scoped impact: offers the maker believed permanently cancelled become fillable again? Proof idea: state-transition test asserting that after maker sets isRootRatified[maker][root]=false, an authorized operator can set it back to true and a take succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an AUTHORIZED OPERATOR ratify a root, have their authorization revoked by the maker (midnight.setIsAuthorized(operator, false, maker)), yet the root remains ratified in isRootRatified[maker][root] under PRECONDITIONS: operator ratifies root, maker revokes operator, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: operator.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> maker.setIsAuthorized(operator, false, maker) -> taker.take(offer, ratifierData, ...), violating INVARIANT: authorization revocation must prevent further unauthorized actions, causing scoped impact: offers remain fillable after the authorizing relationship is terminated? Proof idea: unit test asserting isRootRatified[maker][root] remains true after operator authorization is revoked, and take still succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an AUTHORIZED OPERATOR use Midnight.multicall to atomically (1) call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) via a delegatecall-compatible path and (2) call midnight.take(offer, ratifierData, units, operator, ...) in a single transaction under PRECONDITIONS: operator is authorized by maker, SetterRatifier is authorized as ratifier, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: midnight.multicall([setIsRootRatified_calldata, take_calldata]), violating INVARIANT: multicall must not bypass per-action invariants or enable actions that require separate authorization steps, causing scoped impact: atomic ratify-and-take that maker cannot front-run or prevent? Proof idea: PoC using multicall to batch setIsRootRatified + take in one tx, assert maker debt increases atomically.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an AUTHORIZED OPERATOR exploit the coarse-grained authorization model to grant authorization to a second attacker address (midnight.setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, maker)) and then have the attacker call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) under PRECONDITIONS: operator is authorized by maker (isAuthorized[maker][operator]=true), authorized_address_can_add_or_remove_authorizations=true, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: operator.midnight.setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, maker) -> attacker.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> taker.take, violating INVARIANT: authorization chain must not allow privilege escalation beyond maker's original intent, causing scoped impact: attacker with no direct maker relationship ratifies arbitrary offer trees? Proof idea: three-party test asserting attacker can ratify roots after operator grants them authorization.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: isRatified + HashLib.isLeaf] Can a TAKER submit a Merkle proof with proof.length=0 and leafIndex=0 to ratify a single-offer tree where root == HashLib.hashOffer(offer) under PRECONDITIONS: maker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, hashOffer(offer), true), taker calls take(offer, abi.encode(hashOffer(offer), 0, new bytes32[](0)), units, ...), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: setIsRootRatified -> take with empty proof, violating INVARIANT: ratifier approval must bind correct maker, market, offer, amount, price, deadline, nonce, salt, and consumption group, causing scoped impact: any offer whose hash the maker ratifies as a root can be filled without a multi-leaf tree, potentially enabling unintended single-offer fills? Proof idea: fuzz offer fields, assert empty-proof take succeeds iff isRootRatified[maker][hashOffer(offer)]=true.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: HashLib.isLeaf] Can a TAKER supply a proof array of length >= 256 to bypass the leafIndex bounds check (leafIndex >> proof.length == 0 is always true for uint256 when proof.length >= 256) under PRECONDITIONS: maker has ratified a root, taker constructs a proof of length 256 with arbitrary leafIndex, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: take(offer, abi.encode(root, arbitraryLeafIndex, proof256), units, ...), violating INVARIANT: leafIndex must be within tree bounds, causing scoped impact: attacker can supply out-of-bounds leafIndex values that pass the bounds check, potentially enabling proof forgery if a hash collision is found? Proof idea: fuzz proof.length >= 256 with arbitrary leafIndex, assert LeafIndexOutOfRange is not triggered and verify hash chain correctness.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: HashLib.isLeaf + HashLib.hashNode] Can a TAKER exploit a Merkle second-preimage vulnerability where an internal node hash (hashNode(left, right)) equals the hash of a crafted offer (hashOffer(maliciousOffer)) under PRECONDITIONS: maker ratifies a root for a legitimate tree, attacker finds or constructs a maliciousOffer whose hashOffer equals an internal node of the tree, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker.take(maliciousOffer, abi.encode(root, leafIndex, shorterProof), units, ...), violating INVARIANT: ratifier approval must bind correct maker, market, offer, amount, price, deadline, nonce, salt, and consumption group, causing scoped impact: attacker fills an offer the maker never included in the tree? Proof idea: differential test comparing hashNode output against hashOffer output for crafted inputs, assert no collision is reachable with valid Offer struct encoding.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: HashLib.isLeaf] Can a TAKER use the same Merkle proof with a different leafIndex to prove membership of a different leaf in the same tree under PRECONDITIONS: maker ratifies root for a 2-leaf tree [offerA, offerB], taker has valid proof for offerA at leafIndex=0, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker.take(offerB, abi.encode(root, 1, proof_for_offerA), units, ...), violating INVARIANT: each offer must be independently bound to its position in the tree, causing scoped impact: taker fills offerB using a proof constructed for offerA, bypassing maker's intended offer terms? Proof idea: unit test with 2-leaf tree, assert that swapping leafIndex with the sibling proof does not produce a valid proof for the wrong offer.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Does SetterRatifier lack chain ID binding in its root ratification storage, unlike EcrecoverRatifier which uses block.chainid in its domain separator, under PRECONDITIONS: same SetterRatifier deployed at same address on two chains (e.g., via CREATE2), maker ratifies root on chain A, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: on chain B, taker calls take(offer, abi.encode(root, leafIndex, proof), units, ...) where isRootRatified[maker][root] is also true on chain B (if maker ratified there too), violating INVARIANT: ratifier approval must not be replayable across chains, causing scoped impact: cross-chain replay of root ratification enables unauthorized borrowing on a chain the maker did not intend? Proof idea: differential test comparing SetterRatifier and EcrecoverRatifier chain-binding properties, assert SetterRatifier has no chain ID in its ratification check.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: HashLib.hashOffer] Does HashLib.hashOffer omit chain ID from the offer hash, making SetterRatifier-ratified offers chain-agnostic, under PRECONDITIONS: maker ratifies root on chain A, same offer struct is valid on chain B (same market params, maturity, tick, etc.), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker on chain B submits take with same offer and ratifierData, violating INVARIANT: gates, ratifiers, signatures, callbacks, and authorizations must not be bypassable, causing scoped impact: offer fills on unintended chains if SetterRatifier is deployed at same address cross-chain? Proof idea: inspect hashOffer fields, assert no chainId or INITIAL_CHAIN_ID is included, compare with EcrecoverRatifier's domain separator.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified + Midnight.take] Can a MAKER'S CALLBACK CONTRACT call setIsRootRatified(maker, newRoot, true) during a take callback to ratify a new root, then reenter midnight.take with the new root under PRECONDITIONS: maker's offer has a callback field set to a contract that calls setIsRootRatified and then reenters take, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker.take(offer1) -> maker.callback.onBuy -> setIsRootRatified(maker, newRoot, true) -> midnight.take(offer2, abi.encode(newRoot, ...), ...), violating INVARIANT: multicall/callback/reentrancy must not break invariants that hold for single calls, causing scoped impact: reentrant take fills a second offer that was not ratified at the start of the outer take? Proof idea: PoC with reentrant callback, assert that inner take with newly ratified root succeeds and check debt/credit accounting consistency.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a TAKER'S CALLBACK CONTRACT call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) during a taker callback (onSell) to ratify a root for a subsequent reentrant take under PRECONDITIONS: taker's callback is set to a contract that calls setIsRootRatified and reenters take, maker has authorized SetterRatifier as ratifier, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: taker.take(offer1, ..., takerCallback) -> takerCallback.onSell -> setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> midnight.take(offer2, abi.encode(root, ...), ...), violating INVARIANT: callbacks must not corrupt partial state through reentrancy, causing scoped impact: second take fills offer2 using a root ratified mid-callback, bypassing maker's pre-transaction intent? Proof idea: stateful fuzz test with reentrant taker callback, assert consumed[maker][group] and debt/credit invariants hold after nested takes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an AUTHORIZED OPERATOR front-run a taker's pending take transaction by calling setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) to revoke the root, causing a DoS on the taker's take under PRECONDITIONS: maker has ratified root, taker submits take tx to mempool, operator (authorized by maker) front-runs with revocation, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: operator.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) -> taker.take reverts with NotRatified(), violating INVARIANT: valid offers must remain fillable; low-cost DoS must not block take, causing scoped impact: attacker cheaply blocks take by toggling root ratification? Proof idea: simulate mempool ordering, assert that operator can always front-run any take by revoking root at negligible cost.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can an AUTHORIZED OPERATOR grief a maker by repeatedly toggling isRootRatified[maker][root] between true and false, preventing any taker from reliably filling offers under PRECONDITIONS: operator is authorized by maker, operator monitors mempool and front-runs every take with a revocation, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: repeated operator.setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) before each taker.take, violating INVARIANT: valid offers must remain fillable, causing scoped impact: permanent low-cost DoS on all offers in a ratified root? Proof idea: invariant test asserting that an authorized operator can always prevent takes at gas cost proportional to number of take attempts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take] Can a TAKER fill a sell offer (maker is seller, buy=false) using SetterRatifier where the maker's root was ratified pre-maturity but the take occurs post-maturity, causing a debt increase after maturity under PRECONDITIONS: maker ratifies root pre-maturity, block.timestamp > offer.market.maturity at take time, offer is a sell offer (sellerDebtIncrease > 0), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) at t < maturity -> warp to t > maturity -> taker.take(sellOffer, ratifierData, units, ...), violating INVARIANT: debt must not increase after maturity, causing scoped impact: CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity revert is the expected guard, but verify SetterRatifier adds no bypass? Proof idea: fuzz timestamp around maturity boundary, assert take reverts with CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity when sellerDebtIncrease > 0 post-maturity.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a MAKER ratify a root post-maturity for a sell offer (buy=false) to enable debt-increasing takes after maturity under PRECONDITIONS: block.timestamp > offer.market.maturity, maker calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true), taker calls take(sellOffer, ratifierData, units, ...), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: warp to post-maturity -> setIsRootRatified -> take, violating INVARIANT: debt must not increase after maturity, causing scoped impact: SetterRatifier ratification post-maturity should not bypass CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity check in Midnight.take? Proof idea: unit test asserting take reverts with CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity regardless of when root was ratified.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified + Midnight.take] Can a TAKER bypass an enterGate restriction by using a SetterRatifier-ratified offer where the gate check is applied after ratification under PRECONDITIONS: market has enterGate set, gate blocks taker from increasing debt, maker ratifies root containing sell offer, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> taker.take(sellOffer, ratifierData, units, taker, ...) where gate.canIncreaseDebt(taker) returns false, violating INVARIANT: gate checks must apply to all entry paths, causing scoped impact: SetterRatifier ratification does not bypass gate checks (gate is checked after ratification in take), but verify ordering? Proof idea: unit test with restrictive enterGate, assert take reverts with SellerGatedFromIncreasingDebt even when root is ratified.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a MAKER who authorized SetterRatifier as ratifier (isAuthorized[maker][setterRatifier]=true) but did NOT call setIsRootRatified have their offers filled if isRootRatified[maker][root] defaults to false under PRECONDITIONS: maker authorizes SetterRatifier as ratifier in Midnight, maker never calls setIsRootRatified, taker calls take(offer, abi.encode(root, 0, proof), units, ...), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: midnight.setIsAuthorized(setterRatifier, true, maker) -> taker.take, violating INVARIANT: ratifier must not approve offers without explicit maker consent, causing scoped impact: verify NotRatified() revert is always triggered when isRootRatified[maker][root]=false? Proof idea: unit test asserting take reverts with NotRatified when maker never called setIsRootRatified.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: isRatified] Can a DIRECT CALLER (not Midnight) call isRatified(offer, ratifierData) to observe ratification state without triggering a take under PRECONDITIONS: attacker calls setterRatifier.isRatified(offer, ratifierData) directly (not via Midnight), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: attacker.isRatified(offer, ratifierData), violating INVARIANT: isRatified must only be callable by Midnight, causing scoped impact: NotMidnight() revert is expected, but verify no state leak or side effect occurs from direct call? Proof idea: unit test asserting direct call reverts with NotMidnight(), and that no storage state changes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a MALICIOUS MARKET CREATOR create a market with SetterRatifier as the ratifier for a maker's offer, then ratify a root on behalf of the maker (if authorized) to force the maker into unwanted positions under PRECONDITIONS: market creator is also authorized by maker, market creator calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) for a root containing a sell offer in the new market, trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: createMarket -> setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> taker.take(sellOffer, ratifierData, units, ...), violating INVARIANT: user funds must not be stolen, frozen, or seized outside valid protocol rules, causing scoped impact: maker forced into debt in a market they did not choose? Proof idea: unit test with market creator == authorized operator, assert maker.debt increases in attacker-created market.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified + HashLib.hashOffer] Can a TAKER submit an offer with modified fields (e.g., different maxUnits, tick, or expiry) that still passes isRatified because the Merkle proof is constructed for the modified offer hash under PRECONDITIONS: maker ratifies root for a tree containing offerA, attacker constructs offerB with different maxUnits but same hash (hash collision), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> taker.take(offerB, abi.encode(root, leafIndex, proof_for_offerA), units, ...), violating INVARIANT: ratifier approval must bind correct maker, market, offer, amount, price, deadline, nonce, salt, and consumption group, causing scoped impact: offer terms (maxUnits, tick, expiry) are bypassed via hash collision? Proof idea: differential test asserting hashOffer is collision-resistant for all Offer struct field combinations.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a MAKER ratify a root containing an offer with offer.callback set to an attacker-controlled contract, enabling the attacker to execute arbitrary logic during take under PRECONDITIONS: maker ratifies root for offer with callback=attackerContract, taker calls take(offer, ratifierData, units, ...), trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> taker.take -> attackerContract.onBuy/onSell, violating INVARIANT: callbacks must not corrupt partial state through reentrancy, causing scoped impact: attacker callback reenters Midnight to withdraw collateral or manipulate positions mid-take? Proof idea: PoC with malicious callback that reenters withdrawCollateral, assert health check prevents collateral withdrawal during take.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: setIsRootRatified] Can a MAKER ratify a root containing an offer with offer.group set to a shared consumption group used by other offers, enabling a taker to exhaust the group budget and block other offers under PRECONDITIONS: maker ratifies root for offer with group=sharedGroup, other offers share the same group, taker fills the ratified offer to exhaust consumed[maker][sharedGroup], trigger CALL_SEQUENCE: setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) -> taker.take(offer, ratifierData, maxUnits, ...) -> other takers' takes revert with ConsumedUnits(), violating INVARIANT: consumption group budget cannot be exceeded across offers, causing scoped impact: DoS on other offers sharing the same consumption group? Proof idea: unit test with shared group, assert that filling one offer to maxUnits blocks all other offers in the group.
